common.no_examples_found: No examples found matching your search.
common.no_apps_found: No applications found matching your search.

interactive.title: Interactive Examples
interactive.description: Explore hands-on tutorials, demonstrations, and interactive examples of Reticulum-Go in action.

apps.title: Applications
apps.description: Discover applications and tools built with Reticulum-Go, including Ren Browser and Micron Parser Go.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Browser tools powered by Reticulum-Go and related WASM modules. Cached by the site PWA for offline use after first visit.

micron_editor.title: Micron Editor
micron_editor.description: Live Micron markup editor with WASM preview via Micron-Parser-Go.
micron_editor.page_blurb: Write Micron and see a live HTML preview. Runs entirely in your browser with Micron-Parser-Go WASM.
micron_editor.no_colors: No F/B colors in this page

reticulum_guide.title: Reticulum Guide
reticulum_guide.description: Visual tutorials on destinations, identities, announces, cryptography, packets, links, messaging, and interfaces, grounded in the official Reticulum manual.
reticulum_guide.page_blurb: Interactive explainers that keep the Zen of Reticulum in mind: sovereign networks, destinations not addresses, encryption by default.
reticulum_guide.path_blurb: Work through chapters in order. Each builds on the last.
reticulum_guide.pathfinder_note: PATHFINDER_M is {m}. Hop bytes at or above this value are rejected at unpack.
reticulum_guide.alpha_warning: This guide is alpha. Steps, interactives, and wording may change and may not always be accurate. Prefer the official Reticulum manual and project docs when you need a definitive reference.

rnode_flasher.title: RNODE Flasher
rnode_flasher.description: Flash and provision RNode firmware from the browser. Planned compatibility with tiny-reticulum-go and official RNode firmware builds.

bot_builder.title: Bot Builder
bot_builder.description: Visual builder for LXMF bots powered by LXMFy-Go. Design handlers, destinations, and bot flows without starting from a blank file.

donate.title: Donate
donate.subtitle: Support the development and maintenance of Reticulum-Go.
donate.intro: Choose a platform below or send Monero directly.
donate.meta_description: Support Reticulum-Go development. 50% of donations go to Mark Qvist, creator of the Reticulum reference implementation at reticulum.network.
donate.markqvist_share: 50% of donations go to Mark Qvist, creator of the Reticulum reference implementation.

home.title: Reticulum-Go
home.subtitle: The resilient, sovereign networking stack for everyone. Now implemented in Go for high performance and portability.

encrypted.title: Encrypted by Default
encrypted.description: Reticulum uses strong, modern cryptography end to end. On-wire layouts match the Python reference so peers stay compatible.

resilient.title: Resilient & Sovereign
resilient.description: No central authorities, no servers to shut down. Build your own infrastructure over any medium - radio, fiber, satellite, landlines, or the internet.

go.title: Go Implementation
go.description: Built with Go for portability, auditability, and concurrency. Run it from embedded devices to cloud servers.

interop.title: Full RNS Interoperability
interop.description: Wire-compatible with the official Python Reticulum (RNS). Packets, links, and cryptography work peer-to-peer.

sandbox.title: Secure by Design
sandbox.description: The native daemon enables an OS sandbox by default. The WASM build runs inside the browser sandbox of the host runtime.

modular.title: Modular & Embeddable
modular.description: Use it as a daemon, Go library, C ABI (librns), control API, or WASM module.

coexistence.title: Expanding Reticulum's Reach
coexistence.description: Reticulum-Go was built to work with the Python reference implementation by Mark Qvist, not to replace it. The goal is to help the network and expand where Reticulum can run, while Python RNS remains the reference that defines the protocol.

ren_promo.title: Browse the Reticulum Network
ren_promo.description: Ren Browser is a modern NomadNet-compatible client built with Reticulum-Go. Render Micron pages, manage identities, and explore the mesh from desktop or mobile.

ren_browser.title: Ren Browser
ren_browser.subtitle: A modern browser for the Reticulum Network, built with Reticulum-Go.

nomadnet.title: NomadNet / Micron
nomadnet.description: Render NomadNet pages with full Micron markup support for browsing the mesh.

identities.title: Multiple Identities
identities.description: Create, import, export, and switch between Reticulum identities with ease.

interfaces.title: Interface Control
interfaces.description: Full control over network interfaces with hot reload for live configuration changes.

documents.title: Document Reader
documents.description: Read EPUB and PDF documents alongside mesh content in one place.

editor.title: Micron Editor
editor.description: Write and preview Micron pages with a built-in editor.

extensions.title: Extension System
extensions.description: JS and WASM plugins with a permissions model to extend the browser.

platforms.title: Runs Anywhere
platforms.description: Linux, Windows, macOS, Android, plus Docker and headless server builds.

i18n.title: Localized UI
i18n.description: Interface available in English, German, Spanish, and Russian.

ren_browser.mobile_description: A native-feeling experience on Android with the same identities, discovery, and browsing tools.
ren_browser.get_description: Pre-built packages for Linux, Windows, macOS, and Android, plus a headless server and Docker image.
ren_browser.platforms_app_list: Linux (AppImage, Flatpak, .deb, .rpm), Windows, macOS, Android, iOS
ren_browser.platforms_web_list: Linux (amd64, arm64, armv6), FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD, Windows
ren_browser.note: Active development. Expect rough edges until v1.0.
